post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 264
UIC SSG4



It is the year 10035. With the Theom empire solidly in control of most of the continent of Pacifica, the dynasty has declared peace and prosperity everlasting. But underneath that veneer, there is unease. Dissent against the empire is punished harshly. Those with anima are taken away to be indoctrinated into the empire's army or forced to fight in the Dikhotohm. And slave trade has become blatant, with small villages disappearing entirely overnight. The Myohan in particular, as the architects and builders of the Citadel, force thousands and thousands of slaves to labor in the creation of their projects.

Many people object to the cruelty quickly becoming the new normal. They may not speak openly of their distaste, but many of them have connections. Eventually, a plan to sabotage one of the Myohan's labor camps takes shape. Weapons and explosives are obtained, and a group is formed in secret to infiltrate their way through Myohan's sprawling abandoned cities. However, the Arol have eyes and ears everywhere...


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules for easy reference.

For experienced players: everything you need is here, and in the setup tab.
For new players: you may find it useful to familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside this thread, your role PM, or approved private chats, even if you are dead. Access to spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 12 days while each "Night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses 12:2 Daycount Minimum Deadlines. Refer to the Moderation Policies tab.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to first PM prod, 72 hours to second PM prod, and 96 hours to replacement status.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization does not matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Both bold vote and code vote formats are accepted, although bold is preferred. Refer to the Voting tab if you do not know what these are.
  • Majority hang is always on but cannot end day before the minimum deadline has elapsed.
  • Deadline hang is always on except for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Disagreements should be resolved in a way that does not affect gameplay.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties.
  • All other rules are standard.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Cop9 with N0 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • The Mafia faction and Town PR may be assigned private mentors. Do not talk about mentors in the game thread.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by galaxy1220 and mathjay.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Contact the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your role PM or through Discord (卧槽#5081).
  • For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format to be counted: Vote: [player username].
    • By default, votes should be made in [b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced.
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC).
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 1, if a player is voted by the majority of living players after the minimum deadline has elapsed, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • This game uses 12:2 daycount minimum deadlines, and day phases cannot end before the minimum deadline has elapsed. Details on the minimum deadline lengths are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • galaxy1220 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 264.
    • Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Purple text is used for third-party flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368) and Activity Check blue (#005dff). For a full list of custom colors, click here.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG264 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.
    Current version: 10.8.3 (Minimum Deadlines Standard Non-newbie)
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.
Setup
This game is based on the Cop9 setup. This is an open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. The roles are as follows:

1 Town Alignment Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons

Other important setup mechanics:
  • The cop is given a random Night 0 cop check on one of the Vanilla Townies. This result is always green.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Mafia factional kills (NKs) are not compulsive.
  • Mentors may be assigned to the Town Cop and Mafia faction. Do not talk about mentors in the thread.
Role PMs
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are a Mercenary (Vanilla Townie).

You have been recruited by a nameless organization for a mission deep into the sprawling maze-like ruins surrounding Myohan. Your objective is to sabotage one of their five main labor camps, and you've been briefed on how to get there and where to place the explosives. However, you don't know anyone else on the mission; normally, you would decline a risky job like this, but the pay had been 10 Lars, easily 20 times what you usually make.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Cop Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Encoder (Town Cop).

You have been recruited by a nameless organization for a mission deep into the sprawling maze-like ruins surrounding Myohan. Your objective is to sabotage one of their five main labor camps, and you've been briefed on how to get there and where to place the explosives. You were also given an unnecessarily complicated cypher to learn. You only know how to encode messages and send them by an Upya messenger bird; you don't know how to decode the replies - that was the Decoder's job - and you can't decipher the list of passphrases meant to verify everyone's identity. But now that everyone's said what their passphrase is, you can spend all night encoding one person's passphrase and comparing it to see if it's in the list.

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will check their alignment, finding out whether they are Town or Mafia. You will be informed if your action was blocked. In addition, at the start of the game, you will be given the name of a randomized player who is town.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.
Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are a Empire Mercenary (Mafia Goon).

You and your partners were in a bar when you overheard some mercs boasting drunkenly about a plan to bomb one of the Myohan's slave labor camps. You followed them after they left, and tortured them into giving you the details of the plan before killing them. Two days later, you and two others show up in their place. No one seems to be the wiser, but then one of your partners discovers that you were supposed to have passphrases. He manages to kill the Decoder in time, but is outed and quickly killed by the rest of the party, while the two of you hastily make up bogus passphrases. However, the Encoder is still alive, and will eventually be able to figure it out. Time is ticking...

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.

[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced. 
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC). 
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 1, if a player is voted by the majority of living players after the minimum deadline has elapsed, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]This game uses 12:2 daycount minimum deadlines, and day phases cannot end before the minimum deadline has elapsed. Details on the minimum deadline lengths are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance. 
[*]If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent. 
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p24617357]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]galaxy1220 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 264. 
[*]Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*][color=#9a00ff]Purple text[/color] is used for third-party flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 ([b][color=#2be368]#2be368[/color][/b]) and Activity Check blue ([b][color=#005dff]#005dff[/color][/b]). For a full list of custom colors, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617373]click here[/url]. 
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.[/list]

[b]MG264 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) 10.[/b]
[i]Current version: 10.8.3 (Minimum Deadlines Standard Non-newbie)[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]

[b]OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24637777]click here[/url]. [/b]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/wiki/Cop_Setups]Cop9[/url] setup. This is an open setup. 

In this setup, there are 9 players. The roles are as follows: 

[b]1 Town Alignment Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/b]

Other important setup mechanics: 
[list][*]The cop is given a random Night 0 cop check on one of the Vanilla Townies. This result is always green.  
[*]Cop results are sane. 
[*]Mafia have daychat.
[*]Mafia factional kills (NKs) are not compulsive. 
[*]Mentors may be assigned to the Town Cop and Mafia faction. Do not talk about mentors in the thread. [/list]
[hide=Role PMs]
[quote=Vanilla Townie Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/1083899190947623022/IMG_6512.jpg[/img] 

Hello, [player]!

You are a [b][color=#2be368]Mercenary (Vanilla Townie)[/color][/b]. 

[i]You have been recruited by a nameless organization for a mission deep into the sprawling maze-like ruins surrounding Myohan. Your objective is to sabotage one of their five main labor camps, and you've been briefed on how to get there and where to place the explosives. However, you don't know anyone else on the mission; normally, you would decline a risky job like this, but the pay had been 10 Lars, easily 20 times what you usually make. [/i]

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck. 

[url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]Click here to go to the game thread.[/url]
[/quote]
[quote=Town Cop Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/1083899190947623022/IMG_6512.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Encoder (Town Cop)[/color][/b]. 

[i]You have been recruited by a nameless organization for a mission deep into the sprawling maze-like ruins surrounding Myohan. Your objective is to sabotage one of their five main labor camps, and you've been briefed on how to get there and where to place the explosives. You were also given an unnecessarily complicated cypher to learn. You only know how to encode messages and send them by an Upya messenger bird; you don't know how to decode the replies - that was the Decoder's job - and you can't decipher the list of passphrases meant to verify everyone's identity. But now that everyone's said what their passphrase is, you can spend all night encoding one person's passphrase and comparing it to see if it's in the list. [/i]

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will check their alignment, finding out whether they are Town or Mafia. You will be informed if your action was blocked. In addition, at the start of the game, you will be given the name of a randomized player who is town. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck. 

[url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]Click here to go to the game thread.[/url]
[/quote]
[quote=Mafia Goon Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/1083899190947623022/IMG_6512.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are a [b][color=red]Empire Mercenary (Mafia Goon)[/color][/b]. 

[i]You and your partners were in a bar when you overheard some mercs boasting drunkenly about a plan to bomb one of the Myohan's slave labor camps. You followed them after they left, and tortured them into giving you the details of the plan before killing them. Two days later, you and two others show up in their place. No one seems to be the wiser, but then one of your partners discovers that you were supposed to have passphrases. He manages to kill the Decoder in time, but is outed and quickly killed by the rest of the party, while the two of you hastily make up bogus passphrases. However, the Encoder is still alive, and will eventually be able to figure it out. Time is ticking...[/i]

Your partner is [b][color=red][player] ([role])[/color][/b], and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this private forum[/url] while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill. 

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck. 

[url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]Click here to go to the game thread.[/url]
[/quote]

post_rendered:
The playerlist has been finalized, and role PMs will be sent out tomorrow morning. In the meantime, here's a running recap of what's happened so far with Citadel flavor. Summaries of the events in Citadel 0, 1, and 2 will be posted, and eventually they will be archived in this post.

Citadel 0


Citadel 0.1: Euler
5/16/10520

Euler wakes up in the middle of the Citadel, the walled fortress capital of the Theom empire. He's in the House of Alay, where the Matroness tells him that he's a victim of the Curse, a mysterious condition that causes people to lose their memories. In the Citadel, those affected are arrested and taken away; so the Matroness helps him escape. Aided by her anima Cheris, who can shift into many different forms, Euler breaks into a military barrack to gather supplies. Then he poses as a young, rich nobel in order to gain access to the Dikhotohm, an underground arena where people and their anima are forced to fight for entertainment. Once they reach the lowest level of the Dikhotohm, they blow up a path through the walls and escape into the Catacombs, a sprawling network of tunnels deep underneath the Citadel.


Citadel 0.2: Socoobo
5/16/10520

Socoobo regains consciousness as a servant in the house of Chancellor Giwo Rhaam. With the help of the other servers, he manages to avoid being discovered as an Anull, someone who's lost their memories to the Curse. However, he witnesses the Chancellor publicly executing a member of the emperor's court, who had tried to hide his own amnesia and failed. He encounters trouble trying to go after the Chancellor and gets into a fight, killing a guard but getting injured. As he dramatically proclaims that he was first attacked by the guard, he's pronounced defective by the steward of the house and carted away.


Citadel 0.3: falliinqsnow-
5/24/10520

Snow starts in the Outside, the cluster of scrappy villages and settlements surviving just outside the giant walls of the Citadel. She's in the Redera midday market, on the west side of the Citadel, and she's given some supplies by an old fruit seller. However, she's still confused and unaware that she's Cursed; as a result, she's reported to the guards and arrested. She's busted out by an eccentric Arol assassin named Sana and her anima Ezekiel. They've been monitoring the Anulls - what the empire calls people like Snow who've suddenly lost their memories - trying to figure out where the empire is taking them.


Citadel 0.4 - Mathletesv
5/31/10520

Mathletesv almost drowns. He was on the run from empire mercenaries in the Zezo region when he and his anima Kian jumped off of the Sepani Waterfall. Exhausted, Kian was forced to use up some of Mathletesv's memories in order to gather enough power to break their fall. The two of them try to run, but they're surrounded on the riverbank by soldiers dropped out of the sky by giant teratoms. They fight the soldiers and are able to blind the soldiers long enough to escape further into the Pyo rainforest.

post_rendered:
Long
Big Wallpost Part One: Part Two Coming SoonTM

POSTS 1 TO 100

About (#26), I still believe that saying the meta read method out loud makes the method not work. At least, you can't be sure that it still works, which is basically the same thing as it not working. What's more, meta is already a pretty unstable way of reading, with confounding variables such as how active the thread is, how hard they are being pushed, the other players in the game and their playstyles, and even how they are doing IRL.

While meta might have some credibility before, saying the method out loud in the beginning of a game like this absolutely destroys this credibility, because we'd have no way to know, save for using other reading methods, whether it's the alignment causing playstyle differences, or the confounding variables I mentioned.

Obviously, Wisteria didn't think so, at least at the time of posting. So let's proceed with such assumption. With that assumption, it is perfectly reasonable that Wisteria posts this as town, so this is NAI.

What is not NAI, however, is the fact that when snow didn't respond to Wisteria, Wisteria never follows up. Now, the entire point of Wisteria's post is presumably to be able to read ad333 better, but why bother asking the question if you don't really care whether or not you get an answer? If you ask a question with the mindset of genuinely wanting to read someone better, wouldn't you, like, actually be looking for an answer?

And it couldn't be that Wisteria forgot, since I later commented on it (#34) and she then commented on my comment on it and we had an entire exchange about it, ending somewhere around (#67). From this, I come the conclusion that perhaps Wisteria asked the question not for an answer but simply to get towncred.

On the other hand, snow also didn't respond to Wisteria's post. Perhaps she simply never saw that the question was addressed directly to her. But with ad333 also wanting snow's take (#30), either she's avoiding the question or she is not paying attention at all. The second one is NAI while the first is scummy.

ad333's response to this, however, is towny. He makes a few lighthearted comments like (#54). In particular, though, his comment about leveling his ranges (#40) is what sealed the deal for me townreading ad333 here, because it is unlikely for him to make that post as scum. Let's be honest, scum!ad333 would definitely be more careful.

POSTS 101 TO 200

Finally, things start happening. As I've mentioned previously, posts that andy09 made in this section in response to my attack were not great. Posts like (#136), (#137) are defensive posts with way too vehemence that my attacks warrant, especially the "if you are so keen to read between the lines how can you not understand something as simple as this."

Things get even worse when andy09 suddenly starts to throw personal attacks (#152) and also responding to Socoobo's post against joe as if it were an attack against himself (#157). The personal attack part is somewhat explained when he says that I made personal attacks on him (#177) and later that he only personally attacked me because I did the same. But then, I really can't see what part of my posts could possibly be seen as a personal attack. It would be great if you (andy09) gave some quotes as to which part of what I said was a personal attack.

In any case, his response to Socoobo's post was super self-conscious. It's true that newbtown tends to be this way, but this level of defensiveness is getting a little ridiculous.

Chris's posts in this section were also gross. We have (#134), which basically says that my read is terrible, and then this random post against presumably me, presumably responding to Wisteria saying that I wasn't scummy (#141) and then a quick followup (#145) that not only does not disprove Wisteria's argument that I wasn't scummy, but also is very hypocritical since Chris has not done all that much either. Then, of course, as if that's not enough, another post (#155) saying that my reads suck, and then proceeding to forget about it for the rest of the game. Of course.

The way Chris is suddenly so committed, but also not that committed to attacking me feels like his attacking of me wasnt genuine; that he wasn't actually attacking me for the sake of thinking that I'm scum, but rather for the sake of bandwagon. His post saying that I haven't done anything seemed more of an argument against me being towny than me being not scummy. The only person then who said I was towny was… andy09, whom Chris addressed in third person in his post.

Quick recap, we have the following readlist (each row in no particular order):

ad333
Cosmos (null)
snow
andy Chris Wisteria

Which sucks because there is not 3 scum in the game, contrary to popular opinion.

POSTS 201 TO 300

In here, snow gets significantly scummier, with the "got the response I was looking for" which seemed like a retraction on a read that's way too quick and frantic for my taste (#215) and the self-conscious "Socoobo definitely scumreads me" in (#218). But at least I agree with snow that in no world are snow/Wisteria w/w, from both this attack exchange and from snow ignoring Wisteria's question and Wisteria ignoring snow's ignoring of her question. This puts snow on the same level as the other three, in my opinion. Looking through the other posts, I don't see anything substantial enough to waver my opinions.

INTERACTIONS

Since Cosmos's slot is basically just unresolvable, I will not try to resolve it at all. I'm just gonna say Cosmos town for the sake of the others being scummier. I will look for interactions between the four scumreads (out of SEVEN PLAYERS istg) {snow, andy, Chris, Wisteria} since there's only six of them. We've already established these four as scum, so the rest of this will be focused on interactions rather than individuals.

snow/Wisteria: already addressed — not likely

snow/Chris: there's actually not much interaction going on here. In fact, this pair is so devoid of interaction that it's sad. When Wisteria prompts Chris to comment on Chris, however, he says that snow is a good hang because she's just "existed and glided by" (#280) which is just simply not true and possible an attempt to distance. Definitely a viable pair.

snow/andy: Snow scumread andy in the very beginning, and… continued to do so even after saying that her andy read wasn't really a good one (#195), only based on a toneread, and then after that her mentions of andy are very little, usually in the context of teams (#492) and hangs (#591). The fact that she kind of never really talks about andy makes me think this might be bussing and so it's also a very viable pair.

andy/Chris: When Chris was like "[ming's] literally [just] trying to attack andy" it is possible that Chris and andy are paired. However, voting joe while saying that andy is better (#506) and then switching to an andy vote seems like an interestingly not w/w progression. Idk. Possible pair, but not the most likely. Also, the lord has spoken, guys. The chris doesn't know how to make their own reads.

andy/Wisteria, Chris/Wisteria, Cosmos solution, posts 300+, and details about individual players coming tomorrow.

By the time the sun was nearing the horizon and the half-finished stone monuments and walls, the group had made its way well into the snarled ruins. Everything asw made out of the same dark grey stone, characteristic of the marble mined from the mountain ranges of Myohan, aged by the rain and weather. The amber light of the sunset cast long, slanting shadows that obscured the winding paths through the ruins.

The group decided to rest for the night, climbing a half-finished building and setting up on the roof, where the sky was more visible.

Like all fights, it escalates insidiously in the blink of an eye.

"I'm not going to sleep here where you can just walk over and stab me in the back," someone said quietly, venomously.

By the time the others looked over, andy09 was falling to their death.

"It's just the sky."

"You can't really see it anymore in the cities because of all the light, and the smoke, and everything else. You'd have to really go into the desert, or the mountains where literally no one lives."

"We're in the middle of the ruins."

"We're close to Myohan. They make their slaves work all day, all night; it's how they build so quickly. But every night, for exactly three hours, they turn off all their lights, and you can see the stars, even right in the middle of their capital."

"..."

"..."

"It really is beautiful."

"Heh. I told you."

"By the way, they really would be sneaking around after their partner died. This is for what you did to him."
